---
title: "Global Transit Costs"
author: "Jim Gruman"
date: "1/5/2021"
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    toc: no
    code_folding: hide
runtime: shiny
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, 
                      message = FALSE, 
                      warning = FALSE)
```

```{r, results = FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytuesdayR)
library(scales)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(countrycode)
library(shiny)
library(glue)
})

suppressMessages(
tt <- tt_load("2021-01-05")
)

theme_set(hrbrthemes::theme_ipsum_gs() +
          theme(plot.title.position = "plot", 
                plot.caption.position = "plot"))
```

```{r echo=FALSE}
transit_cost <- tt$transit_cost %>%
  filter(!is.na(e)) %>%
  mutate(real_cost = as.numeric(real_cost)/1000,
         country_code = ifelse(country == "UK", "GB", country),
         country = countrycode(country_code, "iso2c", "country.name"),
         tunnel_per = tunnel / length,
         rr = ifelse(rr, "Railroad", "Not Railroad"),
         station_density = stations / length) %>%
  mutate(country = fct_infreq(country))
metrics <- c("Track Length (km)" = "length",
             "Tunnel Length (km)" = "tunnel",
             "Stations" = "stations")

inputPanel(
  selectizeInput("country", label = "Country:",
                 choices = str_remove(levels(transit_cost$country), "United States"), 
                 selected = "China"),
  selectInput("metric", label = "Metric:", choices = metrics,
              selected = metrics[1])
)

transit_cost_country <- reactive({
  transit_cost %>%
    filter(country %in% c(input$country,"United States"))
})

renderPlotly({
  us_color <- hrbrthemes::ft_pal()(8)[3]
  other_color <- hrbrthemes::ft_pal()(8)[2]

  metric <- input$metric
  x_axis_lab <- names(metrics)[metrics == metric]
  
transit_cost_country() %>%
    mutate(countrycolor = case_when(
      country == "United States" ~ us_color,
      country == input$country ~ other_color,
      TRUE ~ "gray50"
    ),
    suffix = case_when(
      metric == "length" ~ paste0(round(cost_km_millions)),
      metric == "stations" ~ paste0(round(station_density)),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    label = case_when(
      country == "United States" ~ glue("{city} {suffix}"),
      country == input$country ~ glue("{city} {suffix}"),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )) %>% 
    ggplot(aes(!!sym(metric), 
               real_cost, 
               color = countrycolor,
               label = label)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_text_repel(size = 3.5, 
                    segment.color = "#f8f8f2", 
                    segment.alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar) +
    labs(x = x_axis_lab,
         y = "Billion USD",
         color = "Country") +
    theme(legend.position = "",
          plot.title = element_markdown(size = 22,
                                        margin = margin(0, 0, 0.5, 0, unit = "line")),
    plot.subtitle = element_markdown(size = 14, 
                                     lineheight = 1.2, 
                                     margin = margin(0, 0, 1, 0, unit = "line")),
    plot.margin = margin(1.5, 1.5, 1, 1.5, unit = "line"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(1, 0, 0.5, 0, unit = "line")),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "#44475a", size = 0.3),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "#44475a", size = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
          ) +
    labs(title = "Investment in Mass Transit in the 21<sup>st</sup> Century",
         subtitle = glue::glue("The <span style='color:{us_color};'>United States</span> has allocated $57B to 13 urban rail projects, mostly in New York and<br />California. No project exceeds 10 km and the most expensive project costs $11B for 2.8<br />km of railway. In comparison, <span style='color:{other_color};'>{input$country}</span> has allocated over ${ format(sum(transit_cost$real_cost[transit_cost$country == input$country]), digits = 0) }Bn to {sum(transit_cost$country == input$country)} urban rail projects,<br />the longest of which runs {max(transit_cost$length[transit_cost$country == input$country])} km and costs ${round(max(transit_cost$real_cost[transit_cost$country == input$country]),1)}Bn."), 
    y = "Billions of $ US", x = x_axis_lab,
    caption = "Data from transitcosts.com | @jim_gruman")
})

```
